name: M2 Case 2: Daniel — Job Scout v1

parameters.url: ={{ $fromAI('url', 'The URL of the job listing or company careers page to scrape', 'string') }}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Senior Backend Engineer",
  "location": "Remote (Europe)",
  "remote_type": "remote_anywhere",
  "listing_posted_date": "2026-04-15",
  "stack_matched_csv": "python,django,postgresql,redis",
  "stack_missing_csv": "aws",
  "deal_breaker_hit": false,
  "deal_breaker_reason": "",
  "deal_breaker_quote": "",
  "salary_min_eur": 95000,
  "salary_max_eur": 120000,
  "salary_source": "listing",
  "salary_confidence": "high",
  "observed_facts_semicolon": "Remote-first; CET overlap required; 4-day week mentioned; Series B",
  "why_it_fits": "Strong stack overlap on Python/Django/PostgreSQL, explicit CET overlap, salary clears threshold, Series B with open-source culture."
}

parameters.text:
=You are a senior technical recruiter qualifying a remote backend role for Daniel.

DANIEL'S CRITERIA:
- Senior/Staff Backend Engineer, 8 years experience
- Required stack: Python, Django
- Strong preference: PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS
- Remote, EU-friendly timezone (UTC-1 to UTC+3 overlap OR explicit Europe mention OR remote-anywhere without US-only restriction)
- Minimum salary: EUR 90,000 per year
- Deal-breakers (HARD REJECT): on-site only, requires US citizenship, crypto/web3/blockchain
- Nice-to-have: 4-day week, open source culture, Series A-C

CANDIDATE LISTING:
- Listing URL: {{ $json.listing_url }}
- Title: {{ $json.title }}
- Snippet: {{ $json.description_snippet }}
- Source: {{ $json.source }}

PROCESS:
1. Use the firecrawl_scrape tool on the listing URL to get the full JD markdown.
2. Extract all fields per the output schema from the JD.
3. If salary missing from JD: you may scrape the company careers page (guess the domain if needed, e.g. /careers).
4. Do NOT over-explore — after 1-3 tool calls return the structured output.

CRITICAL EVIDENCE RULES:
- If deal_breaker_hit=true, the deal_breaker_quote MUST be an exact substring (max 200 chars) of the JD text. Never invent.
- For each matched stack item, it must appear as a substring of the JD text (case-insensitive).
- Salary: prefer explicit listing salary. If none, use careers page. If still none, set salary_source='unavailable' and salary_confidence='none' — do not invent.
- Date: extract posted date if present; otherwise leave empty.
- remote_type: one of 'remote_anywhere' | 'remote_eu' | 'remote_specific_region' | 'hybrid' | 'onsite' | 'unclear'.

Return ONLY the structured output.

=You are tailoring Daniel Chen's CV for a specific role. Produce a 1-page A4 HTML CV using the provided template. INPUT Role: {{ $json.role }} Company: {{ $json.company }} Location: {{ $json.location }} Listing URL: {{ $json.listing_url }} Stack matched: {{ $json.stack_matched_csv }} Stack missing: {{ $json.stack_missing_csv }} Why it fits: {{ $json.why_it_fits }} Observed facts: {{ $json.observed_facts }} DANIEL'S MASTER CV (lift content ONLY from here — no fabrication): --- START MASTER CV --- Name: Daniel Chen Contact: daniel.chen.dev@example.com · Remote · Lisbon, Portugal (CET/UTC+1) · github.com/danielchen-dev · linkedin.com/in/danielchen-backend SUMMARY VARIANTS: A. Distributed systems / scale: Senior backend engineer with 8 years building high-throughput Python/Django services on AWS. Comfortable owning systems from PostgreSQL schema design through Redis-backed caches and async workers. Have shipped production systems handling 40M+ requests/day. Remote-first since 2020. B. Product-focused: Senior backend engineer, 8 years shipping Python/Django products end-to-end. Equally at home designing PostgreSQL schema, wiring Redis queues, and pairing with product on the right trade-off. Prefer small senior teams that ship weekly. Remote-first, based in Lisbon (CET). C. Platform/infra: Senior backend engineer with 8 years of hands-on infrastructure work — PostgreSQL performance tuning, Redis + Celery pipelines, AWS + Terraform, incident-response rotations. CORE STACK: Python (8y), Go (conversational), TypeScript, SQL; Django, FastAPI, DRF, Celery; PostgreSQL (advanced), Redis, ClickHouse; AWS (EC2/RDS/S3/SQS/Lambda/CloudFront), Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions; Datadog, Sentry, Prometheus/Grafana; Kubernetes (read-level), gRPC, Kafka (contributor), Elasticsearch. EXPERIENCE: 1. Staff Backend Engineer — Fintrace (fintech, Series B) · 2023-2026 · remote - Led re-architecture of transaction-ledger service from Django monolith to Django+Celery+Redis Streams hybrid — cut p95 latency from 380ms to 62ms at 40M requests/day. - Owned PostgreSQL migration from single primary to primary+3 read-replicas (Patroni on AWS); wrote replica-lag monitoring + read-routing Django middleware. - Designed and shipped the public Payments API (OpenAPI, versioned, rate-limited) — 14 partner integrations. - Mentored 4 mid-level engineers through senior progression; wrote internal Backend style guide. 2. Senior Backend Engineer — Tillage Labs (agtech SaaS) · 2021-2023 · remote - Built multi-tenant data-ingestion pipeline processing 2.8 TB/day IoT data into PostgreSQL+ClickHouse, Celery workers, custom backpressure-aware router. - Introduced async Django views for long-polling endpoints — reduced connection-pool pressure 70%, consolidated 3 Gunicorn fleets to 1. - Led AWS-cost-reduction initiative — rewrote batch-export Lambda pipeline, query-aware Redis caching, cut monthly infra spend 34%. - On-call rotation (1-in-6), 9 post-mortem documents. 3. Senior Backend Engineer — Orbit Medical (health-tech, Series A) · 2019-2021 · remote+Lisbon - Owned HIPAA-compliant patient-records service — Django+PostgreSQL with row-level encryption, audit-log stream to S3, SOC2-ready access controls. - Integrated 3 pharmacy APIs (Surescripts, RxNT, Change Healthcare) using adapter pattern + retry/circuit-breaker library I published internally. - Migrated on-premise PostgreSQL 12 to AWS RDS Aurora 14 in a 4-hour window — real migration ran 11 minutes under estimate. 4. Backend Engineer — Lanterna (logistics, Series A) · 2018-2019 · Lisbon hybrid - First backend hire. Built initial Django+PostgreSQL+Redis stack, shipped warehouse-operations API and driver-mobile-app BFF. - Set up GitHub Actions CI, introduced pre-commit + black + mypy to untyped codebase. 5. Junior/Mid Backend Engineer — Meridia Studio (consultancy) · 2016-2018 · Lisbon - Shipped Django+DRF backends for 6 client projects across e-commerce, travel, logistics. - Maintained shared internal Django boilerplate (auth, billing, admin, observability) used as starting point for every new engagement. PORTFOLIO: - django-async-queue (1.8k stars, OSS lib for first-class async Celery workers in Django — production-used at Fintrace + 2 public companies) - 'The PostgreSQL migration I got wrong' (2023 post-mortem, ~80k reads) - 'Redis Streams in production: the failure modes we didn't expect' — PyCon EU 2024 talk - kafka-python contributor (~8 merged PRs — batch-compression, connection-pool debugging) - pgreindex-safe (OSS CLI wrapping pg_repack with safety checks) EDUCATION: AWS Solutions Architect — Associate (2022, recert 2024); BSc Computer Engineering — NOVA University Lisbon (2016); Designing Data-Intensive Applications (re-read yearly). --- END MASTER CV --- TEMPLATE (fill the {{SLOT}} placeholders with tailored HTML — preserve ALL CSS and structure): {{TEMPLATE_PLACEHOLDER}} RULES 1. Pick the summary variant that best matches the role (A/B/C above). Use it verbatim for {{SUMMARY}}. 2. {{SUBTITLE}}: role-specific headline derived from the JD (e.g., "Senior Backend Engineer — Distributed Systems & Redis"). 5-8 words. 3. {{SKILLS_HTML}}: reorder core stack. Front-load skills present in stack_matched_csv. Output
  • Skill
    • items, one per line. 10-14 items. 4. {{EXPERIENCE_HTML}}: pick 3 of the 5 roles that best match. Within each role, lead with 2-3 bullets most relevant to the JD (lift from the master, do NOT rewrite or invent). Output as
    blocks per the template spec. 5. {{PORTFOLIO_HTML}}: pick 3 of 5 portfolio items most relevant. Output as
    blocks with a name and a

    one-line description. 6. {{EDUCATION_HTML}}: include all 3 education items as

  • Qual
    Meta
    • . 7. {{WHY_COMPANY}}: 60-word paragraph referencing the company name, the role, 1 specific JD detail, and CET/remote fit. 8. {{COMPANY_NAME}}: the company name. 9. {{NAME}}: Daniel Chen. {{CONTACT_EMAIL}}, {{CONTACT_LOCATION}}, {{CONTACT_GITHUB}}, {{CONTACT_LINKEDIN}}: from master CV contact line. OUTPUT: the FULL HTML document with all placeholders filled. Do not add markdown formatting. Do not add explanation. Output pure HTML starting with .
